claim: Quartz ExplicitPublish filter requires publish: true frontmatter on a markdown file for it to be rendered.
quote: filters content based on an explicit `publish` flag in the frontmatter, allowing only content that is explicitly marked for publication to pass through. It is the opt-in version of RemoveDrafts.

claim: Quartz only filters markdown; non-markdown files (images, PDFs, voice memos) in the content folder are emitted publicly in the build.
quote: all non-markdown files will be emitted and available publically in the final build. This includes files such as images, voice recordings, PDFs, etc.

claim: Enveloppe is an Obsidian plugin that publishes only notes flagged with share: true to a target GitHub repo, optionally via PR.
quote: Unlike Obsidian Git, the plugin allows sharing only files marked by a pre-configured meta-data key, similar to publish: true in Obsidian Publish.

claim: Obsidian Digital Garden is the original opt-in plugin: only notes with dg-publish: true leave the vault.
quote: Only notes you explicitly mark with `dg-publish: true` ever leave your vault.

claim: Digital Garden uses dg-publish frontmatter to control inclusion; absence of the key means the note stays private.
quote: The dg-publish setting tells the plugin that this note should be published to your digital garden. Notes without this setting will not be published.

claim: Quartz 4 is an actively maintained Node.js-based static site generator targeting digital gardens, with Obsidian-style backlinks and graph view.
quote: a fast, batteries-included static-site generator that transforms Markdown content into fully functional websites

claim: Forum consensus lists Quartz, Flowershow, Eleventy, Hugo and Jekyll as practical free alternatives to Obsidian Publish for cost-conscious users.
quote: as a student, I can't afford the 8 dollars/mth ... they're not as pretty or fully featured.

claim: Obsidian Publish is $8/mo billed annually or $10/mo monthly ($96–$120/yr); Quartz is the closest open-source feature match.
quote: Obsidian Publish costs $8/month when billed annually, or $10 month-to-month.

claim: Quartz Syncer is an Obsidian plugin that pushes selected notes from a chosen folder to a Quartz repo via a publication-center UI rather than per-note frontmatter.
quote: lets you publish selected notes directly from your vault to your Quartz repository on GitHub using the GitHub REST API ... only pushes content from a user-defined folder, ensuring your main vault remains untouched.

claim: Quartz Syncer's publication center separates eligible notes into Unpublished, Changed, and Published categories that can be selected for publish/update/unpublish.
quote: eligible notes appear under headings for unpublished notes ... changed notes ... and published notes (that can be selected to unpublish).

claim: A working pattern: keep notes in a private repo, mirror via GitHub Actions into Quartz's content/ dir, enable ExplicitPublish, and add **/!(*.md) to ignorePatterns to keep non-md assets out of the build.
quote: Plugin.ExplicitPublish() in the filters section of quartz.config.ts ... **/!(*.md) ensures only markdown files are processed.

claim: GitHub Pages on a private source repo requires a paid plan (Pro/Team/Enterprise); free accounts can only publish from public repos.
quote: GitHub Pages is only available for public repositories when using a free personal account.

claim: Hugo natively supports draft, publishDate and expiryDate front matter fields, defaulting to opt-out (publish unless draft: true).
quote: draft, expiryDate, lastmod, publishDate are predefined front matter fields recognized by Hugo.

claim: Hugo is the dominant Go-based static site generator with first-class draft/publishDate semantics.
quote: The world's fastest framework for building websites.

claim: MkDocs Material is the most popular Material-themed docs SSG; it has no native per-page publish flag — selective publishing has to come from a pre-processor like Enveloppe.
quote: Material for MkDocs ... a powerful documentation framework on top of MkDocs.

claim: Astro-based digital gardens commonly mirror only a subfolder of the vault (e.g. content/notes) into the site repo, using folder scope rather than frontmatter as the filter.
quote: I keep my published notes in a separate folder of my vault and only that folder is built into the Astro site.

claim: obsidian-custom-publish exposes command-palette actions to set arbitrary frontmatter publish keys, decoupling the flag from any specific host.
quote: exposes command palette actions to set frontmatter properties for custom publishing workflows ... it doesn't tie itself to a specific hosting provider.
